integration.processDes: The System process metricset provides process statistics. One document is provided for each process.
integration.containerDes: The container metricset provides container information and status statistics.
integration.negateDes: Defines whether the pattern is negated.
integration.multilineAllRequired: The value must be filled in
integration.metricNetworkDes: The System network metricset provides network IO metrics collected from the operating system. One event is created for each network interface.
integration.auditBeatPathsDes: List of directory paths that need to be monitored
integration.startPatternTips:
Match the starting line of a new log event using regular expressions to distinguish between independent log entries. 
Typical Application Scenarios: 
1. Java Logs: Starting with a timestamp or log level 
2. Docker Logs: Matching specific prefixes of container logs (such as [2022-10-01]) 
3. SQL Query: Identifying the starting keyword of an SQL statement (such as SELECT)
integration.haltBefore: Keep merging until encountering a line that matches the condition (but not including that line). For example, stop when seeing a new log timestamp, ensuring the timestamp belongs to the next log. Suitable when new logs have clear starting markers.
integration.healthcheckDes: The healthcheck metricset provides container health check status statistics.
integration.uptimeDes: The System uptime metricset provides the uptime of the host operating system.
integration.containerPlaceholder: Please enter the container and press Enter to end
integration.continueThrough: Continue merging all lines that match the condition until encountering a non-matching line. For example, in Java error stack traces, all lines starting with spaces will be merged into the previous log. Suitable for scenarios with clear 'subordinate line' characteristics.
integration.eventDes: The event metricset provides Docker event statistics.
integration.dhcpPortDes: DHCP port configuration, including server ports and client ports.
integration.flowsTimeoutDes: Traffic timeout time (in seconds), connections that exceed this time are considered to have ended.
integration.docherNetworkDes: The network metricset provides container network traffic statistics.
integration.matchDes: Specifies how Filebeat combines matching lines into an event.
integration.haltWith: Merge up to and including the first line that matches the condition. For example, when logs end with a semicolon ;, it will include the line with the semicolon to indicate the end. Suitable for cases with clear end markers.
integration.conditionValidate: Tags, conditions, or values cannot be empty.
integration.timeoutMsTips: Parameter used to control multi-line merge timeout in log processing, in milliseconds (ms). It defines the maximum waiting time for the system to wait for the next matching line. When this time threshold is exceeded, the buffer content will be forcibly flushed for output even if the current log message is incomplete.
integration.processSummaryDes: The process_summary metricset collects high level statistics about the running processes.
integration.raidDes: This is the raid metricset of the module system. It collects stats about the raid.
integration.socketSummaryDes: The System socket_summary metricset provides the summary of open network sockets in the host system.It collects a summary of metrics with the count of existing TCP and UDP connections and the count of listening ports.
integration.intervalDes: The time interval for collecting monitoring data (unit: seconds).
integration.fsstatDes: The System fsstat metricset provides overall file system statistics.
integration.conditionPatternTips:
Use regular expressions to determine whether subsequent logs should be merged into the current log event. 
Typical application scenarios:
1. Java stack trace: an exception stack using ^s+ matching scaling. 
2. Continuation character scenario: \\$ matches a line ending with a back slash (indicating that the next line is a continuation of the current line). 
3. Terminator scenario: ;$ Match lines ending with semicolons (indicating the end of the log)
integration.sampleRateDes: It can be adjusted according to network traffic to reduce data volume.
integration.fileSystemDes: The System filesystem metricset provides file system statistics. For each file system, one document is provided.
integration.networkSummaryDes: The network_summary metricset provides network connection summary statistics.
integration.memoryDes: The System memory metricset provides memory statistics.
integration.ruleCategoriesDes: Preset rule categories to simplify configuration
integration.loadDes: The System load metricset provides load statistics.
integration.flowsPeriodDes: Traffic statistics cycle (seconds), outputting statistical information every other time.
integration.serviceDes: The service metricset reports on the status of systemd services.
integration.meetRule: Meet the following conditions, such as
integration.infoDes: The info metricset provides Docker daemon information statistics.
integration.cpuDes: The System cpu metricset provides CPU statistics.
integration.coreDes: The System core metricset provides usage statistics for each CPU core.
integration.maxLinesDes: The maximum number of lines that can be combined into one event. If the multiline message contains more than max_lines, any additional lines are discarded.
integration.diskIoDes: The System diskio metricset provides disk IO metrics collected from the operating system. One event is created for each disk mounted on the system.
integration.continuePast: Stop when encountering lines that don't match the condition, but capture one additional line. For example, when logs end with a backslash \, it will merge both the line with \ and the next line. Suitable for cases where you know you need to read one more line after the end marker.
integration.timeoutDes: After the specified timeout, Filebeat sends the multiline event even if no new pattern is found to start a new event. The default is 5s.
integration.patternDes: Depending on how you configure other multiline options, lines that match the specified regular expression are considered either continuations of a previous line or the start of a new multiline event. You can set the negate option to negate the pattern.
integration.multilineDes: Merge the collected logs according to corresponding rules and conditions
integration.modeTips: Defines the aggregation logic for log lines, which determines when the merge will stop based on the rules of start pattern and merge regex.
integration.imageDes: The image metricset provides Docker image information statistics.
integration.socketDes: The system socket metricset reports an event for each new TCP socket that it sees. It does this by polling the kernel periodically to get a dump of all sockets.
integration.belongingGroupTips: Receive instance viewing and operations, and isolate permissions according to their organization.
integration.logGroupTips: The effective log grouping scope of this strategy
integration.k8sNote: K8S only supports active reporting, and is not supported to be accessed here for the time being.

k8s.prerequisitesDesc: Before starting, make sure the following requirements are met:
k8s.presetHint: Start with Standard K8s. Switch to Docker only when nodes still use Docker, and use Custom only when the real node log path differs from the presets.
k8s.resourceRequirement: Each cluster node should reserve enough resources for the log collector
k8s.accessAssetDesc: Choose "Create New Asset" to create a new Kubernetes log access instance, or choose an existing instance to regenerate the install command.
k8s.clusterNameDesc: Used to distinguish log access instances for different Kubernetes clusters. Use a readable and meaningful name.
k8s.organizationDesc: Choose the organization that owns this cluster. Only members of the selected organization can view and manage its logs.
k8s.k8sClusterDesc: Select an existing Kubernetes log access instance to regenerate its install command.
k8s.cloudRegionDesc: Select the cloud region where the Kubernetes cluster resides. Logs will be reported through the region's NATS service.
k8s.runtimeProfileDesc: Choose the preset that matches the node log layout. The default preset mounts /var/log; the Docker preset also mounts /var/lib/docker/containers; use custom only when your environment differs.
k8s.hostLogPathDesc: Vector kubernetes_logs must be able to read the node Pod log directory, commonly /var/log/pods or /var/log.
k8s.dockerContainerLogPathDesc: Fill this only when nodes still use Docker and you need the raw container log directory, commonly /var/lib/docker/containers.
k8s.absolutePathRequired: Please enter an absolute path starting with /
k8s.installCommandDesc: Run the following command on a Kubernetes node with kubectl configured to install the log collector:
k8s.verifyWaitingDesc: After running the command, wait for logs to be reported before verification.
k8s.verifySuccessDesc: Kubernetes logs have been detected. The cluster is now connected to the log system.
k8s.verifyFailedDesc: No cluster logs have been detected yet. Please check the
k8s.accessCompleteDesc: Your Kubernetes cluster has been successfully connected to the log system.
k8s.accessCompleteSubDesc: The system is collecting cluster logs. You can now start browsing and searching log data.
k8s.commonIssuePendingReason: This is usually caused by insufficient resources or scheduling restrictions such as taints.
k8s.commonIssuePendingSolution1: Check node resource usage: kubectl top nodes
k8s.commonIssuePendingSolution2: Check scheduling events for the DaemonSet/Pod: kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n bk-lite-collector
k8s.commonIssueNatsReason: This is usually caused by network issues, invalid certificates, or incorrect cloud region NATS settings.
k8s.commonIssueNatsSolution1: Check collector logs: kubectl logs -n bk-lite-collector -l app=vector-daemonset
k8s.commonIssueNatsSolution2: Verify that the cloud region NATS address, username, password, and CA certificate are correct
k8s.commonIssueMountTitle: Collector is running but no logs arrive
k8s.commonIssueMountReason: This is usually caused by a host log mount that does not match the actual node layout. For example, Vector needs access to /var/log/pods but the chosen preset mounted the wrong directory.
k8s.commonIssueMountSolution1: Switch the log path preset in access settings. If the environment is special, choose custom and fill in the real node log path.
k8s.commonIssueMountSolution2: Inspect the collector container: kubectl exec -it -n bk-lite-collector <vector-pod> -- sh, then verify /etc/vector/vector.yaml and the target log directory exist.

integration.snmpTrapStep1Des: Configure the following target address on the device to send Trap messages
integration.snmpTrapStep2Des: Obtain the MIB files from the device vendor and place them in the following directory on the node
integration.snmpTrapNodeDes: Please select the node that has been hosting the snmptrapd program
integration.syslogStep1Des: Please configure the following Syslog server address in your device or application to send log messages to this receiver
integration.syslogNodeDes: Please select the container node that has been hosting the Telegraf collector
integration.syslogStep1: Configure Syslog Target Address on Device
integration.endpointPlaceholder: Docker daemon connection address, default is unix:///var/run/docker.sock
integration.accessLogDesc: Collect Apache access logs, recording detailed information for each HTTP request
integration.errorLogDesc: Collect Apache error logs, recording server errors, configuration issues, module warnings, etc.
integration.nginxAccessLogDesc: Collect Nginx access logs, recording detailed information for each HTTP request
integration.nginxErrorLogDesc: Collect Nginx error logs, recording server errors, configuration issues, upstream connection failures, etc.
integration.ingressControllerLogDesc: Collect Kubernetes Ingress Controller logs, recording routing and processing information for ingress traffic
integration.mysqlErrorLogDesc: Collect MySQL error logs, recording database startup, runtime errors, warnings, etc.
integration.mysqlSlowLogDesc: Collect MySQL slow query logs, analyzing SQL statements with long execution times. Enable slow query in MySQL first: slow_query_log=ON
integration.postgresqlServerLogDesc: Collect PostgreSQL server logs, recording database connections, query execution, error warnings, etc.
integration.redisServerLogDesc: Collect Redis server logs, recording startup, client connections, persistence, replication, etc.
integration.redisSlowLogDesc: Retrieve Redis slow query records via SLOWLOG command in real-time, no file output required. Set slowlog-log-slower-than threshold first
integration.redisPasswordHint: Redis AUTH password (if requirepass is enabled)
integration.mongodbServerLogDesc: Collect MongoDB server logs, recording database operations, replica set status, performance metrics, etc.
integration.kafkaLogDesc: Collect Kafka Broker logs, including server logs, Controller logs, state change logs, etc.
integration.kafkaLogPathHint: One path per line, supports glob patterns
integration.rabbitmqServerLogDesc: Collect RabbitMQ server logs, recording startup, connections, channels, queues and other core events
integration.esServerLogDesc: Collect Elasticsearch server logs, recording cluster status, node events, index operations, etc.
integration.esGcLogDesc: Collect JVM garbage collection logs. Ensure GC log output is configured when starting ES: -Xlog:gc*:file=/path/gc.log
integration.esAuditLogDesc: Collect Elasticsearch audit logs, recording security-related access and operation events
integration.esSlowLogDesc: Collect Elasticsearch slow query and slow index logs. Configure cluster slow log threshold first: index.search.slowlog.threshold.query.warn
integration.esDeprecationLogDesc: Collect Elasticsearch deprecation warning logs, recording usage of deprecated APIs and features
integration.winlogSecurityLogDesc: Login/logout, permission changes, audit policy and other security events
integration.winlogSystemLogDesc: System components, drivers, service start/stop and other system events
integration.winlogApplicationLogDesc: Application runtime logs, errors and warnings
integration.winlogSysmonLogDesc: Sysmon advanced security monitoring events (requires Sysmon installation)
integration.winlogPowershellLogDesc: PowerShell script execution and command logs
integration.winlogSecurityEventIdHint: Common events: 4624 (login success) 4625 (login failed) 4672 (privileged login) 4688 (process creation) 4720 (user creation)
integration.winlogIgnoreOlderDesc: Ignore events older than specified time, reducing initial sync data volume
integration.dockerEndpointTooltip: Docker API connection address, default is unix:///var/run/docker.sock
integration.dockerContainerFilterDesc: Filter containers to collect by name
integration.dockerContainerNameContainsTooltip: Only collect containers with matching names
integration.dockerContainerPlaceholder: Press Enter to add, e.g.: nginx
integration.dockerMultilineMergeDesc: Merge multiline logs (e.g., Java stack traces, Python Traceback) into one event
integration.dockerMergeModeTooltip:
Determines the multiline log merge strategy

continue_through: Continue merging subsequent lines that match, suitable for Java stack traces, Python Traceback

continue_past: Current line matches, merge next line, suitable for Shell line continuation \

halt_before: Stop when subsequent line doesn't match, suitable for timestamp-prefixed logs

halt_with: Stop when subsequent line matches, suitable for SQL ending with semicolon
integration.dockerMatchRegexTooltip:
Lines matching this regex will be processed according to the merge mode

Common examples:

^[\s]+: Match lines starting with whitespace, for Java/Python stack continuations

^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}: Match lines starting with date, use with halt_before

^[?\d{4}: Match lines starting with [2024 or 2024, for timestamped logs

^(ERROR|WARN|INFO|DEBUG): Match lines starting with log level

\\$: Match lines ending with backslash, use with continue_past for continuations
integration.dockerStartPatternTooltip: Matches the start of a log event, lines not matching will be merged into the previous event
integration.dockerTimeoutMsTooltip: Maximum time to wait for next line, buffer content will be flushed after timeout
integration.httpPortsPlaceholder: Enter port and press Enter, e.g.: 80
integration.httpCaptureBodyHint: Record request and response body content when enabled
integration.captureBodyTooltip: Whether to capture HTTP request and response body content, enabling this allows deeper analysis but increases storage consumption
integration.httpTrafficCaptureDesc: Capture HTTP request and response traffic, parsing request method, URL, status code, response time, etc.
integration.flowsTrafficCaptureDesc: Capture TCP/UDP network flows, recording session source/destination addresses, ports, bytes and duration
integration.flowsStatisticsPeriodHint: Time window for traffic statistics, in seconds
integration.flowsTimeoutHint: Timeout for inactive connections, in seconds
integration.filePathConfigDesc: Configure log file paths to collect, supports glob patterns
integration.filePathsHint:
One path per line, supports glob patterns
Example: /var/log/app/*.log
/var/log/messages
integration.fileExcludePathsHint:
Excluded file path patterns, one path per line
Example: /var/log/app/*.gz
/var/log/app/archive/*
integration.fileLogFormatDesc: Select log format to optimize parsing, enable multiline merge for stack traces
integration.fileParserHint: Select message parsing type, currently only supports JSON
integration.fileMultilineDesc: Merge multiline logs (e.g., Java stack traces, Python Traceback) into one event
integration.fileAdvancedConfigDesc: Configure read position, file encoding and other advanced options
integration.fileReadFromHint: Where to start reading the file on first collection
integration.fileEncodingHint: Character encoding of the log file
integration.fileIgnoreOlderSecsHint: Ignore files with modification time older than specified seconds, default 86400 seconds (1 day)
integration.filePathsPlaceholder: Enter path and press Enter, e.g.: /var/log/app/*.log
integration.fileExcludePathsPlaceholder: Enter path and press Enter, e.g.: /var/log/app/*.gz
integration.fileIntegrityPathConfigDesc: Specify files or directories to monitor for changes
integration.fileIntegrityMonitorPathsHint: List of paths to monitor for file integrity, supports directories and files
integration.fileIntegrityMonitorPathsPlaceholder: Enter path and press Enter, e.g.: /etc/passwd
integration.fileIntegrityExcludePathsHint: Path patterns to exclude from monitoring, will be mapped to regex
integration.fileIntegrityExcludePathsPlaceholder: Enter path and press Enter, e.g.: /etc/*.bak
integration.fileIntegrityMonitorConfigDesc: Configure hash algorithm and monitoring options
integration.fileIntegrityHashAlgorithmHint: Algorithm for calculating file hash, SHA256 recommended
integration.fileIntegrityRecursiveHint: Whether to recursively monitor files in subdirectories

search.searchPlaceHolder: Enter your search query here and press Enter to search

packetbeat.protocolTrafficDistributionDesc: Statistics on traffic ratios generated by different protocols (TCP/UDP/ICMP) to help understand network usage. (Unit: GB)
packetbeat.topDestinationIPsDesc: Displays the top 20 source IPs with the highest traffic for detecting high-volume or anomalous sources. (Traffic unit: GB)
packetbeat.totalNetworkTrafficDesc: Shows network traffic changes over time, helping administrators quickly identify traffic peaks and anomalies. (Unit: GB)
packetbeat.totalPacketCountDesc: Shows total network packets over time, facilitating monitoring of network load and instantaneous peaks.
packetbeat.topSourceIPsDesc: Shows the top 20 source IP addresses with the highest traffic, facilitating discovery of high-traffic sources or potential anomalous sources. (Traffic unit: GB)
packetbeat.highTrafficSankeyDesc: Shows the combination of source IP + destination IP + protocol + source port + destination port with the highest traffic, facilitating locating traffic conditions of specific sessions. (Shows top 20, traffic unit: GB)

event.noticeWay: There is currently no notification channel available. You can go to the
event.closeTitle: Do you want to close this item?
event.setPeriod: Please select the detection period, which is used to measure the duration within which monitoring data is collected.
event.polymerizationConditionsDes: Please select fields for aggregation, such as selecting the source field for aggregation. The source: 1.1.1.1 and source: 1.1.1.2 in the original log will be calculated separately as two alarm policies, each generating events and alarms
event.displayFieldsDes: Please select the display fields in the alarm details table for the alarms generated in the log, in order to obtain more information for alarm analysis.
event.keywordAlertDes: Alert based on keywords according to query criteria
event.notifierTagsPlaceholder: Enter notifier and press Enter to add
event.activeAlarmsMapTips: Display a map of the distribution of all active alarms that meet the filtering criteria.
event.queryCriteriaTips: Please fill in the query criteria. Once the query criteria are met, an alarm will be generated
event.conditionValidate: Functions, fields, conditions, or thresholds cannot be empty
event.closeContent: After closing, the data cannot be recovered.
event.historicalAlarmsMapTips: Displays a graph of the distribution of all historical alarms that match the filter for the selected time range.
event.setFrequency: Please select the detection frequency, which indicates how often to collect monitoring data.
event.keyWordAlertNameTitle: Please enter the corresponding name for the generated alarm.
event.aggregationAlertDes: Alert based on log aggregation results
event.alertNameTitle: Please fill in the corresponding alarm name for the generated alarm, which can reference aggregated fields as variables, such as (source).
event.notificationDesc: Select the notification method and recipients when an alert is triggered.
